Account.description:
Bank account with balance and metadata.

Property names follow Berlin Group PSD2 `accountDetails` where they
overlap (`iban`, `bban`, `bic`, `maskedPan`, `product`, `status`,
`usage`), so servers fronting a PSD2 backend can map fields directly.
Servers SHOULD populate at least one of `accountNumber`, `iban`,
`bban`, or `maskedPan`.

accountNumber.description: Display-friendly account number in the locally familiar format. For European accounts this is typically the IBAN; otherwise the domestic account number. Use the typed identifier fields (`iban`, `bban`, `maskedPan`) when you need to parse or route on it.
accountNumber.title: Accountnumber

availableBalance.description: Funds available to spend right now in `currency`, after pending authorisations and including any `overdraftLimit` headroom. Aligns with the PSD2 `interimAvailable` balance type.
availableBalance.title: Availablebalance

balance.description: Current booked balance in `currency`. For credit accounts a negative value indicates an outstanding balance owed.
balance.title: Balance

balanceUpdatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which `balance` and `availableBalance` were last refreshed by the bank.
balanceUpdatedAt.title: Balanceupdatedat

balances.description: Typed balance entries when the bank exposes more than just the booked and available scalars (`ClosingBooked`, `Expected`, `InterimAvailable`, `ForwardAvailable`, `NonInvoiced`). When populated, the top-level `balance` and `availableBalance` scalars are derived shortcuts for the most-recent `ClosingBooked` and `InterimAvailable` entries; servers MUST keep them consistent. Servers without typed balance support SHOULD omit this field and emit only the scalars.
balances.title: Balances

bban.description: Basic Bank Account Number, the domestic identifier used in countries (or for products) without an IBAN.
bban.title: Bban

bic.description: BIC / SWIFT code of the holding bank (ISO 9362).
bic.title: Bic

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the account. For multi-currency accounts (per PSD2 convention) use `XXX`.
currency.title: Currency

iban.description: IBAN (ISO 13616), when the account has one.
iban.title: Iban

isDefaultAccount.description: Whether this is the user's default account for new transfers. At most one account per response SHOULD have this set to true.
isDefaultAccount.title: Isdefaultaccount

isWithdrawalAccount.description: Whether this account can be used as the source of an outgoing transfer or withdrawal.
isWithdrawalAccount.title: Iswithdrawalaccount

maskedPan.description: Masked Primary Account Number for card accounts. The middle digits MUST be obscured.
maskedPan.title: Maskedpan

minimumPaymentDue.description: Credit accounts only. Minimum payment required by `paymentDueDate` to keep the account current and avoid late fees, in `currency`. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.
minimumPaymentDue.title: Minimumpaymentdue

name.description: Human-readable account name set by the bank or the user.
name.title: Name

openedDate.description: Date the account was opened, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).
openedDate.title: Openeddate

overdraftLimit.description: Approved overdraft on a current/savings account or credit limit on a credit/card account, in `currency`. `0` (or omitted) means no extra headroom beyond `balance`.
overdraftLimit.title: Overdraftlimit

ownerName.description: Primary account holder's name as the bank holds it. For joint accounts, servers MAY join multiple names with `, `.
ownerName.title: Ownername

paymentDueDate.description: Credit accounts only. ISO 8601 date by which `minimumPaymentDue` must be paid. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.
paymentDueDate.title: Paymentduedate

product.description: Bank's marketing name for the account product, per PSD2 `product`. Free-form and proprietary to the issuer.
product.title: Product

statementBalance.description: Credit accounts only. Total billed on the most recent closed statement, in `currency`. Pay this in full by `paymentDueDate` to avoid interest charges. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.
statementBalance.title: Statementbalance

statementClosingDate.description: Credit accounts only. ISO 8601 date the current billing cycle closes and the next statement is generated. Optional even on credit accounts; servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.
statementClosingDate.title: Statementclosingdate

status.description: Lifecycle status. If omitted, clients SHOULD treat the account as `Enabled`.

usage.description: Whether this is a personal or business account.

AccountType.description:
Type of bank account.

Aligned with the ISO 20022 ExternalCashAccountType1Code values exposed
via the Berlin Group PSD2 `cashAccountType`, collapsed to the buckets
that map cleanly across regions and reflect distinctions a user would
actually ask about:

  - `Current`  - day-to-day transaction account (ISO 20022 CACC).
                 Debit cards and stored-value / prepaid cards live
                 here too: `maskedPan` flags an attached card and the
                 balance/availableBalance pair answers "can I use it?"
  - `Savings`  - interest-bearing savings (ISO 20022 SVGS)
  - `Credit`   - revolving credit / credit card account; carries the
                 statement-cycle fields below (`statementBalance`,
                 `minimumPaymentDue`, `paymentDueDate`, ...).
  - `Loan`     - amortizing loan or mortgage (ISO 20022 LOAN)
  - `Other`    - anything else (escrow, brokerage, etc.)

AccountUsage.description:
How the account is used, per Berlin Group PSD2 `usage`.

`Private` corresponds to PSD2 `PRIV` (personal); `Business` to `ORGA`
(professional / organisation).

TransferSummary.description:
Validated, normalized echo of the prepare-transfer inputs.

The user-facing confirmation surface: clients should render this
so the user can confirm before `execute-transfer` is called.

creditor.description: Validated creditor record. The `accountIdentifier` field carries the routing identifier; servers MUST populate it.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the instructed amount.
currency.title: Currency

debtorAccount.description: Resolved snapshot of the debtor (source) account.

description.description: Free-text shown on the counterparty's statement. Falls back to `remittanceInformation.unstructured` when both are present.
description.title: Description

endToEndId.description: ISO 20022 cross-rail identifier. Server-generated when the client did not supply one; populated either way so the client can show / audit the value.
endToEndId.title: Endtoendid

localInstrument.description: Rail-specific local instrument code (e.g., `INST` for SEPA Instant).

requestedExecutionDate.title: Requestedexecutiondate

TransferWarning.description:
Server-emitted advisory about a prepared transfer.

Used for AML hits, sanctions matches, unusual-amount notices, etc.
`severity: "block"` indicates the server will refuse `execute-transfer`
against this intent unless the user explicitly overrides.
TransferWarning.title: TransferWarning

TransferWarningSeverity.title: TransferWarningSeverity

PreparedTransfer.description:
Result of a successful `prepare-transfer` call.

`transferIntentId` is the opaque token the client passes to
`execute-transfer`. `summary` is what the user confirms; everything
else is supporting metadata (fees, FX, payee verification, warnings).

confirmationOfPayee.description: Payee verification outcome. Servers on rails that support Confirmation of Payee / Verification of Payee MUST populate this when known. Servers on rails that do not SHOULD omit.

estimatedSettlement.description: ISO 8601 estimated settlement time at the creditor.
estimatedSettlement.title: Estimatedsettlement

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp after which `execute-transfer` will reject the intent. Servers SHOULD set this 5 minutes ahead of the prepare time; rails MAY shorten or extend.
expiresAt.title: Expiresat

fees.description: Fees the bank will charge for this transfer.
fees.title: Fees

fx.description: FX details when the transfer crosses currencies.

summary.description: Validated echo of the prepare-transfer inputs; the user-facing confirmation surface.

warnings.description: Advisory or blocking warnings emitted during validation.
warnings.title: Warnings

AccountType.description:
Type of bank account.

Aligned with the ISO 20022 ExternalCashAccountType1Code values exposed
via the Berlin Group PSD2 `cashAccountType`, collapsed to the buckets
that map cleanly across regions and reflect distinctions a user would
actually ask about:

  - `Current`  - day-to-day transaction account (ISO 20022 CACC).
                 Debit cards and stored-value / prepaid cards live
                 here too: `maskedPan` flags an attached card and the
                 balance/availableBalance pair answers "can I use it?"
  - `Savings`  - interest-bearing savings (ISO 20022 SVGS)
  - `Credit`   - revolving credit / credit card account; carries the
                 statement-cycle fields below (`statementBalance`,
                 `minimumPaymentDue`, `paymentDueDate`, ...).
  - `Loan`     - amortizing loan or mortgage (ISO 20022 LOAN)
  - `Other`    - anything else (escrow, brokerage, etc.)

TransactionCode.description:
ISO 20022 BankTransactionCode taxonomy.

Three-level hierarchy (`domain` / `family` / `subFamily`). Servers
MAY emit only `domain` if that is all the bank exposes; the deeper
levels are populated when known.

family.description: Mid-level family (e.g., `RCDT` for received credit transfers).
family.title: Family

TransactionStatus.description:
Booking status of a transaction, per Berlin Group PSD2 `bookingStatus`.

`Booked` is the default a client should assume when servers omit
`Transaction.status`.
TransactionStatus.title: TransactionStatus

Transaction.description:
Financial transaction with date, amount and metadata.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `EntryDetails2`, Berlin Group PSD2
`transactions` array element, and Open Finance card transactions
(`cardTransactions`). One shape covers both account and card
transactions: the `_card` family of fields (`transactionDate`,
`maskedPan`, counterparty.postalAddress, `merchantCategoryCode`,
`proprietaryBankTransactionCode`) is populated for card entries,
the `_sepa` family (`mandateId`, `creditorId`, `purposeCode`,
`endToEndId`, `remittanceInformation`) for credit-transfer /
direct-debit entries. Servers MAY omit any optional field; the
`_Bank2aiModel` base drops `None` keys on the wire so every
row stays lean for LLM consumption while a full audit view is
available via `get-transaction`.

`date` carries the most relevant time-anchor for the entry: the
booking date when posted, the authorisation date for pending
card authorisations. This diverges from ISO 20022 / Berlin Group
field naming (`bookingDate`) on purpose — keeping a single,
always-populated date field is friendlier for LLM consumption and
means pending entries don't need a fabricated booking date.

accountId.description: References the `id` of the `Account` this transaction belongs to.
accountId.title: Accountid

additionalInformation.description: Free-form additional information the bank attaches to the entry and which does not fit any of the typed fields above (Berlin Group / Open Finance `additionalInformation`). Servers SHOULD omit when it would duplicate `description` or `remittanceInformation`.
additionalInformation.title: Additionalinformation

amount.description: Transaction amount in the user's default currency (negative for expenses, positive for income). Clients SHOULD render this without a currency symbol unless the user has explicitly asked which currency a transaction is in.
amount.title: Amount

categoryId.description: Category id (the `id` field from get-categories which also has category name).
categoryId.title: Categoryid

categoryRaw.description: Bank-native category label as the upstream system exposes it. Useful when `categoryId` is mapped from something more specific.
categoryRaw.title: Categoryraw

counterparty.description: Typed counterparty record carrying the merchant or counterparty name and any structured detail the bank exposes (account identifier, BIC, national id, address). At `minimal` verbosity this is suppressed and clients fall back to `description`, which for most entries already embeds the counterparty name. For card transactions the merchant address (Open Finance `cardAcceptorAddress` — `townName`, `country`, `postCode`, `streetName`) goes on `counterparty.postalAddress`.

creditorId.description: SEPA Creditor Identifier for direct-debit collections. Profile of: ISO 20022 `CreditorSchemeIdentification`.
creditorId.title: Creditorid

date.description: Primary date for this transaction, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). For `Booked` entries this is the date the transaction posted to the account (formerly `bookingDate`; profile of ISO 20022 / Berlin Group `bookingDate`). For `Pending` authorisations — where no booking date exists yet — this is the authorisation / point-of-sale date (Open Finance `transactionDate`). Always populated so clients can sort and chart by `date` without branching on `status`.
date.title: Date

description.description: Transaction description, merchant name or recipient name
description.title: Description

endToEndId.description: ISO 20022 cross-rail end-to-end identifier preserved across the payment chain.
endToEndId.title: Endtoendid

entryReference.description: Stable bank-side entry reference (Berlin Group `entryReference`). Distinct from `id` in that some banks expose an opaque server-scoped `id` but also carry the raw entry reference from the booking system; useful for delta-fetch / reconciliation.
entryReference.title: Entryreference

mandateId.description: SEPA / direct-debit mandate identifier the bank associates with this entry. Profile of: ISO 20022 `MandateIdentification`.
mandateId.title: Mandateid

maskedPan.description: Masked Primary Account Number of the card used for the transaction. Useful for distinguishing which physical / virtual card a charge belongs to when the same account has multiple cards attached. Servers MUST mask the middle digits.
maskedPan.title: Maskedpan

merchantCategoryCode.description: ISO 18245 Merchant Category Code (4 digits). Card-payment entries SHOULD carry this when the acquirer exposes it; useful for spend analytics and merchant classification.
merchantCategoryCode.title: Merchantcategorycode

originalAmount.description: Transaction amount in `originalCurrency` (negative for expenses, positive for income). Present only when `originalCurrency` is set.
originalAmount.title: Originalamount

originalCurrency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the original transaction, present only when the transaction was made in a currency other than the user's default. Pair with `originalAmount` to recover the original amount.
originalCurrency.title: Originalcurrency

proprietaryBankTransactionCode.description: Bank-proprietary transaction code (Open Finance `proprietaryBankTransactionCode`). Free-form string the bank uses to classify the entry when an ISO 20022 `transactionCode` isn't exposed.
proprietaryBankTransactionCode.title: Proprietarybanktransactioncode

purposeCode.description: ISO 20022 ExternalPurposeCode (4-letter) indicating the purpose of the payment, when the bank exposes it. Distinct from the BankTransactionCode taxonomy on `transactionCode`.
purposeCode.title: Purposecode

remittanceInformation.description: Free-text and/or structured remittance info attached to the transaction. Servers SHOULD omit when it would duplicate `description`.

status.description: Booking status. If omitted, clients SHOULD treat the transaction as `Booked`.

transactionCode.description: ISO 20022 BankTransactionCode classification of the entry.

transactionDate.description: Card swipe / authorisation date for `Booked` card entries where it differs from the posting `date` (Open Finance `transactionDate`). Servers SHOULD omit when equal to `date`, and MUST omit on `Pending` entries — for those, the authorisation date is carried by `date` itself.
transactionDate.title: Transactiondate

valueDate.description: Date the funds become available, ISO 8601. Servers SHOULD omit when equal to `date`. Profile of: ISO 20022 `ValueDate`.
valueDate.title: Valuedate

Transaction.title: Transaction

Bank account with balance and metadata.

Property names follow Berlin Group PSD2 `accountDetails` where they
overlap (`iban`, `bban`, `bic`, `maskedPan`, `product`, `status`,
`usage`), so servers fronting a PSD2 backend can map fields directly.
Servers SHOULD populate at least one of `accountNumber`, `iban`,
`bban`, or `maskedPan`.

accountNumber.description: Display-friendly account number in the locally familiar format. For European accounts this is typically the IBAN; otherwise the domestic account number. Use the typed identifier fields (`iban`, `bban`, `maskedPan`) when you need to parse or route on it.

anyOf.description:
Type of bank account.

Aligned with the ISO 20022 ExternalCashAccountType1Code values exposed
via the Berlin Group PSD2 `cashAccountType`, collapsed to the buckets
that map cleanly across regions and reflect distinctions a user would
actually ask about:

  - `Current`  - day-to-day transaction account (ISO 20022 CACC).
                 Debit cards and stored-value / prepaid cards live
                 here too: `maskedPan` flags an attached card and the
                 balance/availableBalance pair answers "can I use it?"
  - `Savings`  - interest-bearing savings (ISO 20022 SVGS)
  - `Credit`   - revolving credit / credit card account; carries the
                 statement-cycle fields below (`statementBalance`,
                 `minimumPaymentDue`, `paymentDueDate`, ...).
  - `Loan`     - amortizing loan or mortgage (ISO 20022 LOAN)
  - `Other`    - anything else (escrow, brokerage, etc.)

availableBalance.description: Funds available to spend right now in `currency`, after pending authorisations and including any `overdraftLimit` headroom. Aligns with the PSD2 `interimAvailable` balance type.

balance.description: Current booked balance in `currency`. For credit accounts a negative value indicates an outstanding balance owed.

balanceUpdatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which `balance` and `availableBalance` were last refreshed by the bank.

items.description:
One typed balance entry on an account.

Profile of: Berlin Group PSD2 `balances` array element.

asOf.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this balance was last refreshed.

balances.description: Typed balance entries when the bank exposes more than just the booked and available scalars (`ClosingBooked`, `Expected`, `InterimAvailable`, `ForwardAvailable`, `NonInvoiced`). When populated, the top-level `balance` and `availableBalance` scalars are derived shortcuts for the most-recent `ClosingBooked` and `InterimAvailable` entries; servers MUST keep them consistent. Servers without typed balance support SHOULD omit this field and emit only the scalars.

bban.description: Basic Bank Account Number, the domestic identifier used in countries (or for products) without an IBAN.

bic.description: BIC / SWIFT code of the holding bank (ISO 9362).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the account. For multi-currency accounts (per PSD2 convention) use `XXX`.

iban.description: IBAN (ISO 13616), when the account has one.

isDefaultAccount.description: Whether this is the user's default account for new transfers. At most one account per response SHOULD have this set to true.

isWithdrawalAccount.description: Whether this account can be used as the source of an outgoing transfer or withdrawal.

maskedPan.description: Masked Primary Account Number for card accounts. The middle digits MUST be obscured.

minimumPaymentDue.description: Credit accounts only. Minimum payment required by `paymentDueDate` to keep the account current and avoid late fees, in `currency`. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.

name.description: Human-readable account name set by the bank or the user.

openedDate.description: Date the account was opened, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

overdraftLimit.description: Approved overdraft on a current/savings account or credit limit on a credit/card account, in `currency`. `0` (or omitted) means no extra headroom beyond `balance`.

ownerName.description: Primary account holder's name as the bank holds it. For joint accounts, servers MAY join multiple names with `, `.

paymentDueDate.description: Credit accounts only. ISO 8601 date by which `minimumPaymentDue` must be paid. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.

product.description: Bank's marketing name for the account product, per PSD2 `product`. Free-form and proprietary to the issuer.

statementBalance.description: Credit accounts only. Total billed on the most recent closed statement, in `currency`. Pay this in full by `paymentDueDate` to avoid interest charges. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.

statementClosingDate.description: Credit accounts only. ISO 8601 date the current billing cycle closes and the next statement is generated. Optional even on credit accounts; servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.

anyOf.description: Lifecycle status of an account, per Berlin Group PSD2 `accountStatus`.

status.description: Lifecycle status. If omitted, clients SHOULD treat the account as `Enabled`.

anyOf.description:
How the account is used, per Berlin Group PSD2 `usage`.

`Private` corresponds to PSD2 `PRIV` (personal); `Business` to `ORGA`
(professional / organisation).

usage.description: Whether this is a personal or business account.

tools.description: Get transaction categories. Returns a list of categories that transactions can be classified into.
tools.name: get-categories

outputSchema.description: Envelope for a list of categories

items.description:
Transaction category for spending classification.

bank2ai-defined categorization model; not profiled from a single
upstream standard. Localized names live on this object so clients
can render category labels per the user's locale; programmatic
identity goes through `id`. See `CANONICAL_CATEGORY_IDS` for the
recommended id values shared across servers.

id.description: Unique category identifier. SHOULD be one of the canonical ids in `CANONICAL_CATEGORY_IDS` when a server's category maps cleanly; otherwise free-form server-specific.

name.description: Category name (localized)

tools.description: Get saved payment recipients filtered by name. Returns matching recipients with their account details.

name.description: Free-text search; matches partial names of saved recipients.

tools.name: get-recipients

outputSchema.description: Envelope for a list of recipients

items.description:
Saved payment recipient.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `Creditor` and `CreditorAccount` (subset).
Account routing goes through the typed `accountIdentifier`
discriminated union (IBAN, BBAN, country-specific account number,
or alias); national identification is opaque to bank2ai and lives
in the typed `nationalId` sub-object.

accountIdentifier.description: Typed account identifier for routing the transfer.

oneOf.description: IBAN-routed account, ISO 13616.
Domestic Basic Bank Account Number, used in markets without an IBAN.

bban.description: Domestic account identifier in the country's native format.

oneOf.description: Country-specific composite for non-IBAN markets (US, UK pre-IBAN, etc.).

accountNumber.description: Account number in the country's native format.

routing.description: US ABA routing number, when applicable.

oneOf.description: Alias-based identifier (UPI VPA, Pix key, email-routed payments, etc.).

alias.description: The alias value as the user knows it.

bic.description: BIC / SWIFT code of the recipient's bank, ISO 9362.

defaultDescription.description: Pre-filled free-text shown on the recipient's statement for transfers prepared against this recipient. Clients MAY use this as the default `description` when preparing a transfer; users can override per transfer.

isFavorite.description: Whether this recipient is marked as favorite.

lastUsedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the most recent transfer to this recipient.

name.description: Recipient's full name or business name.

anyOf.description: Person or business national identifier.

Opaque label for the kind of national identifier carried in `NationalId.value`.

bank2ai does not validate national-ID formats; this is a hint so
clients can render or route appropriately. Servers SHOULD set the
closest-matching value or use `other`.

type.description: Hint for the kind of identifier. bank2ai does not validate the value.

value.description: National identifier value, in the country's native format.

nickname.description: Optional user-friendly handle for the recipient.

tools.description: Look up a single transaction by id and return every field the server can populate, including ISO 20022 / SEPA metadata (transactionCode, remittanceInformation, endToEndId, mandateId, creditorId, purposeCode, entryReference) and card-side fields (transactionDate, maskedPan, merchantCategoryCode, counterparty.postalAddress). Use this for audit / reconciliation flows; for compact lists prefer `get-transactions` with a `verbosity` cap.

account_id.description: Source account.id (from get-accounts). Optional; servers MAY require it for routing or for additional authorization checks.

tools.name: get-transaction

outputSchema.description:
Result of looking up a single transaction by id.

Returned by the `get-transaction` tool. The envelope mirrors the
recoverable-error pattern used elsewhere in bank2ai: when the
transaction is found `item` is populated and `content` is a brief
status message; when it isn't, `item` is omitted and `content`
explains why.

anyOf.description:
Financial transaction with date, amount and metadata.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `EntryDetails2`, Berlin Group PSD2
`transactions` array element, and Open Finance card transactions
(`cardTransactions`). One shape covers both account and card
transactions: the `_card` family of fields (`transactionDate`,
`maskedPan`, counterparty.postalAddress, `merchantCategoryCode`,
`proprietaryBankTransactionCode`) is populated for card entries,
the `_sepa` family (`mandateId`, `creditorId`, `purposeCode`,
`endToEndId`, `remittanceInformation`) for credit-transfer /
direct-debit entries. Servers MAY omit any optional field; the
`_Bank2aiModel` base drops `None` keys on the wire so every
row stays lean for LLM consumption while a full audit view is
available via `get-transaction`.

`date` carries the most relevant time-anchor for the entry: the
booking date when posted, the authorisation date for pending
card authorisations. This diverges from ISO 20022 / Berlin Group
field naming (`bookingDate`) on purpose — keeping a single,
always-populated date field is friendlier for LLM consumption and
means pending entries don't need a fabricated booking date.

accountId.description: References the `id` of the `Account` this transaction belongs to.

additionalInformation.description: Free-form additional information the bank attaches to the entry and which does not fit any of the typed fields above (Berlin Group / Open Finance `additionalInformation`). Servers SHOULD omit when it would duplicate `description` or `remittanceInformation`.

amount.description: Transaction amount in the user's default currency (negative for expenses, positive for income). Clients SHOULD render this without a currency symbol unless the user has explicitly asked which currency a transaction is in.

categoryId.description: Category id (the `id` field from get-categories which also has category name).

categoryRaw.description: Bank-native category label as the upstream system exposes it. Useful when `categoryId` is mapped from something more specific.

anyOf.description:
Counterparty in a transaction or transfer.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `PartyIdentification135` (subset). For card
transactions the counterparty is the card-accepting merchant;
`postalAddress` then carries the Open Finance `cardAcceptorAddress`.
Discriminated union of typed account identifiers. Profile of: ISO 20022 `AccountIdentification4Choice`.

oneOf.description: IBAN-routed account, ISO 13616.
Domestic Basic Bank Account Number, used in markets without an IBAN.

bban.description: Domestic account identifier in the country's native format.

oneOf.description: Country-specific composite for non-IBAN markets (US, UK pre-IBAN, etc.).

accountNumber.description: Account number in the country's native format.

routing.description: US ABA routing number, when applicable.

oneOf.description: Alias-based identifier (UPI VPA, Pix key, email-routed payments, etc.).

alias.description: The alias value as the user knows it.

bic.description: BIC / SWIFT code of the party's bank, ISO 9362.

name.description: Party's full name or business name.

anyOf.description: Person or business national identifier.

Opaque label for the kind of national identifier carried in `NationalId.value`.

bank2ai does not validate national-ID formats; this is a hint so
clients can render or route appropriately. Servers SHOULD set the
closest-matching value or use `other`.

type.description: Hint for the kind of identifier. bank2ai does not validate the value.

value.description: National identifier value, in the country's native format.

anyOf.description:
Postal address of a party or card-acceptor merchant.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `PostalAddress24` (subset). Aligns with the
Open Finance / Berlin Group `cardAcceptorAddress` structure so card
transactions can surface where the swipe happened. Servers SHOULD
populate whichever subfields the bank exposes and omit the rest;
`townName` and `country` are the most commonly available and the
most useful for LLM context.

addressLine.description: Free-form address lines, used when the bank exposes the address as unparsed text. Servers SHOULD prefer the typed fields above and fall back to `addressLine` only when they cannot decompose the address.

countrySubDivision.description: State, province, region, or other top-level subdivision.

streetName.description: Street name without the building number.

postalAddress.description: Postal address of the party. For card transactions, this is the merchant / card-acceptor address (Open Finance `cardAcceptorAddress`). Servers SHOULD populate whatever subset they have, typically at least `townName` and `country`.

counterparty.description: Typed counterparty record carrying the merchant or counterparty name and any structured detail the bank exposes (account identifier, BIC, national id, address). At `minimal` verbosity this is suppressed and clients fall back to `description`, which for most entries already embeds the counterparty name. For card transactions the merchant address (Open Finance `cardAcceptorAddress` — `townName`, `country`, `postCode`, `streetName`) goes on `counterparty.postalAddress`.

creditorId.description: SEPA Creditor Identifier for direct-debit collections. Profile of: ISO 20022 `CreditorSchemeIdentification`.

date.description: Primary date for this transaction, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD). For `Booked` entries this is the date the transaction posted to the account (formerly `bookingDate`; profile of ISO 20022 / Berlin Group `bookingDate`). For `Pending` authorisations — where no booking date exists yet — this is the authorisation / point-of-sale date (Open Finance `transactionDate`). Always populated so clients can sort and chart by `date` without branching on `status`.

description.description: Transaction description, merchant name or recipient name

endToEndId.description: ISO 20022 cross-rail end-to-end identifier preserved across the payment chain.

entryReference.description: Stable bank-side entry reference (Berlin Group `entryReference`). Distinct from `id` in that some banks expose an opaque server-scoped `id` but also carry the raw entry reference from the booking system; useful for delta-fetch / reconciliation.

mandateId.description: SEPA / direct-debit mandate identifier the bank associates with this entry. Profile of: ISO 20022 `MandateIdentification`.

maskedPan.description: Masked Primary Account Number of the card used for the transaction. Useful for distinguishing which physical / virtual card a charge belongs to when the same account has multiple cards attached. Servers MUST mask the middle digits.

merchantCategoryCode.description: ISO 18245 Merchant Category Code (4 digits). Card-payment entries SHOULD carry this when the acquirer exposes it; useful for spend analytics and merchant classification.

originalAmount.description: Transaction amount in `originalCurrency` (negative for expenses, positive for income). Present only when `originalCurrency` is set.

originalCurrency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the original transaction, present only when the transaction was made in a currency other than the user's default. Pair with `originalAmount` to recover the original amount.

proprietaryBankTransactionCode.description: Bank-proprietary transaction code (Open Finance `proprietaryBankTransactionCode`). Free-form string the bank uses to classify the entry when an ISO 20022 `transactionCode` isn't exposed.

purposeCode.description: ISO 20022 ExternalPurposeCode (4-letter) indicating the purpose of the payment, when the bank exposes it. Distinct from the BankTransactionCode taxonomy on `transactionCode`.

anyOf.description:
Free-text and/or structured remittance information for a transaction.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `RemittanceInformation`. Servers SHOULD populate
this only when remittance info is genuinely present and not just a
duplicate of `Transaction.description`.

creditorReference.description: Structured creditor reference, typically an ISO 11649 RF-prefixed reference. The reference is opaque to bank2ai; servers pass it through as the bank exposes it.

unstructured.description: Free-text remittance line (e.g., the SEPA `RemittanceInformation/Unstructured` field).

remittanceInformation.description: Free-text and/or structured remittance info attached to the transaction. Servers SHOULD omit when it would duplicate `description`.

anyOf.description:
Booking status of a transaction, per Berlin Group PSD2 `bookingStatus`.

`Booked` is the default a client should assume when servers omit
`Transaction.status`.

status.description: Booking status. If omitted, clients SHOULD treat the transaction as `Booked`.

anyOf.description:
ISO 20022 BankTransactionCode taxonomy.

Three-level hierarchy (`domain` / `family` / `subFamily`). Servers
MAY emit only `domain` if that is all the bank exposes; the deeper
levels are populated when known.

family.description: Mid-level family (e.g., `RCDT` for received credit transfers).

transactionCode.description: ISO 20022 BankTransactionCode classification of the entry.

transactionDate.description: Card swipe / authorisation date for `Booked` card entries where it differs from the posting `date` (Open Finance `transactionDate`). Servers SHOULD omit when equal to `date`, and MUST omit on `Pending` entries — for those, the authorisation date is carried by `date` itself.

valueDate.description: Date the funds become available, ISO 8601. Servers SHOULD omit when equal to `date`. Profile of: ISO 20022 `ValueDate`.

tools.description: Get bank transactions. Returns a list of transactions with amounts, dates, descriptions, and categories. The `verbosity` parameter caps how many optional fields the server populates: use `minimal` for compact lists, `standard` (default) for general use, `full` for an audit / reconciliation view including ISO 20022 metadata when the server can populate it.

account_ids.description: Restrict to transactions on these account.id values (from get-accounts).

category_ids.description: Restrict to these category ids (the `id` field from get-categories).

count.description: Maximum number of transactions to return.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned as `nextCursor` from a previous call. Omit to fetch the first page.

max_amount.description: Inclusive upper bound on the transaction amount. Amounts are signed: expenses are negative, income is positive. Examples: `max_amount=0` keeps only expenses; `max_amount=-50` keeps only expenses of 50 or more (in absolute value).

min_amount.description: Inclusive lower bound on the transaction amount. Amounts are signed: expenses are negative, income is positive. Examples: `min_amount=0` keeps only income; `min_amount=-100` drops expenses larger than 100.

verbosity.description: Upper bound on optional fields each Transaction may carry. `minimal` keeps only the required fields (`id`, `accountId`, `description`, `amount`, `date`); the merchant / counterparty name comes from `description`, which for most bank entries already embeds it. `standard` adds the fields an LLM typically needs to answer everyday questions: `status`, `categoryId`, `originalCurrency`, `originalAmount`, `transactionDate`, `maskedPan`, `merchantCategoryCode`, and the typed `counterparty` (name plus merchant address — `townName`, `country`, `postCode`, `streetName`). `full` additionally allows every audit / reconciliation field: `valueDate`, `categoryRaw`, `transactionCode`, `proprietaryBankTransactionCode`, `remittanceInformation`, `endToEndId`, `mandateId`, `creditorId`, `purposeCode`, `entryReference`, `additionalInformation`. Servers MAY omit any optional field even at `full` if they don't have it.

tools.description: Get an aggregated summary of transactions, scoped to either income or expenses. Returns totals, counts, and averages, optionally grouped by category, month, or both. Filters mirror get-transactions: account, date, amount range, category ids.

account_ids.description: Restrict to transactions on these account.id values (from get-accounts).

category_ids.description: Restrict to these category ids (the `id` field from get-categories).

min_amount.description: Inclusive lower bound on the transaction amount. Amounts are signed: expenses are negative, income is positive. Combined with `direction`, both filters are applied.

oneOf.description: Country-specific composite for non-IBAN markets (US, UK pre-IBAN, etc.).

accountNumber.description: Account number in the country's native format.

routing.description: US ABA routing number, when applicable.

oneOf.description: Alias-based identifier (UPI VPA, Pix key, email-routed payments, etc.).

alias.description: The alias value as the user knows it.

bic.description: BIC / SWIFT code of the party's bank, ISO 9362.

name.description: Party's full name or business name.

anyOf.description: Person or business national identifier.

Opaque label for the kind of national identifier carried in `NationalId.value`.

bank2ai does not validate national-ID formats; this is a hint so
clients can render or route appropriately. Servers SHOULD set the
closest-matching value or use `other`.

type.description: Hint for the kind of identifier. bank2ai does not validate the value.

value.description: National identifier value, in the country's native format.

anyOf.description:
Postal address of a party or card-acceptor merchant.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `PostalAddress24` (subset). Aligns with the
Open Finance / Berlin Group `cardAcceptorAddress` structure so card
transactions can surface where the swipe happened. Servers SHOULD
populate whichever subfields the bank exposes and omit the rest;
`townName` and `country` are the most commonly available and the
most useful for LLM context.

addressLine.description: Free-form address lines, used when the bank exposes the address as unparsed text. Servers SHOULD prefer the typed fields above and fall back to `addressLine` only when they cannot decompose the address.

countrySubDivision.description: State, province, region, or other top-level subdivision.

streetName.description: Street name without the building number.

postalAddress.description: Postal address of the party. For card transactions, this is the merchant / card-acceptor address (Open Finance `cardAcceptorAddress`). Servers SHOULD populate whatever subset they have, typically at least `townName` and `country`.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the instructed amount.

description.description: Free-text shown on the counterparty's statement. Falls back to `remittance_information.unstructured` when both are set.

end_to_end_id.description: Optional client-supplied ISO 20022 cross-rail identifier. Servers MUST generate one when the client omits it; the resolved value is echoed in the response summary.

idempotency_key.description: Optional idempotency key, scoped to this tool. Servers SHOULD return the original prepared-transfer response for repeat calls with the same key within at least 24 hours.

local_instrument.description: Rail-specific instrument code; `INST` for SEPA Instant, `RTGS` for SWIFT, etc. Free-form per rail.

rail.description: Settlement rail. Drives validation, fees, and the set of meaningful `local_instrument` values.

anyOf.description:
Free-text and/or structured remittance information for a transaction.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `RemittanceInformation`. Servers SHOULD populate
this only when remittance info is genuinely present and not just a
duplicate of `Transaction.description`.

creditorReference.description: Structured creditor reference, typically an ISO 11649 RF-prefixed reference. The reference is opaque to bank2ai; servers pass it through as the bank exposes it.

unstructured.description: Free-text remittance line (e.g., the SEPA `RemittanceInformation/Unstructured` field).

remittance_information.description: Structured / unstructured remittance information to attach to the transfer.

requested_execution_date.description: ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD) requested execution date. Omit for as-soon-as-possible per the rail.

tools.name: prepare-transfer

outputSchema.description:
Envelope for `prepare-transfer`. Recoverable-error pattern: when
validation succeeded `item` is populated; when it failed `content`
explains why and `item` is omitted.

actions.description: Optional follow-up actions the client may surface.

items.description: Suggested follow-up action for a prepared transfer.

link.description: Target URL or in-app link to perform the action.

code.description: Server-defined code identifying a recoverable error (e.g., `insufficient_funds`, `invalid_account`, `missing_creditor_identifier`). Omitted on success.

anyOf.description:
Result of a successful `prepare-transfer` call.

`transferIntentId` is the opaque token the client passes to
`execute-transfer`. `summary` is what the user confirms; everything
else is supporting metadata (fees, FX, payee verification, warnings).
Result of a payee-name verification against the destination account.

suggestedName.description: On a `close-match` or `no-match`, the actual name on the destination account when the rail returns it.

confirmationOfPayee.description: Payee verification outcome. Servers on rails that support Confirmation of Payee / Verification of Payee MUST populate this when known. Servers on rails that do not SHOULD omit.

estimatedSettlement.description: ISO 8601 estimated settlement time at the creditor.

expiresAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp after which `execute-transfer` will reject the intent. Servers SHOULD set this 5 minutes ahead of the prepare time; rails MAY shorten or extend.

items.description: One fee line item that will apply to the transfer.

fees.description: Fees the bank will charge for this transfer.

anyOf.description: Foreign-exchange details for a cross-currency transfer.

lockedUntil.description: ISO 8601 timestamp until which the quoted rate is held.

sourceAmount.description: Amount debited from the debtor account.

fx.description: FX details when the transfer crosses currencies.

summary.description: Validated echo of the prepare-transfer inputs; the user-facing confirmation surface.

creditor.description: Validated creditor record. The `accountIdentifier` field carries the routing identifier; servers MUST populate it.

anyOf.description: Discriminated union of typed account identifiers. Profile of: ISO 20022 `AccountIdentification4Choice`.

oneOf.description: IBAN-routed account, ISO 13616.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `PostalAddress24` (subset). Aligns with the

transactions can surface where the swipe happened. Servers SHOULD
populate whichever subfields the bank exposes and omit the rest;

addressLine.description: Free-form address lines, used when the bank exposes the address as unparsed text. Servers SHOULD prefer the typed fields above and fall back to `addressLine` only when they cannot decompose the address.

countrySubDivision.description: State, province, region, or other top-level subdivision.

streetName.description: Street name without the building number.

postalAddress.description: Postal address of the party. For card transactions, this is the merchant / card-acceptor address (Open Finance `cardAcceptorAddress`). Servers SHOULD populate whatever subset they have, typically at least `townName` and `country`.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the instructed amount.

debtorAccount.description: Resolved snapshot of the debtor (source) account.

accountNumber.description: Display-friendly account number in the locally familiar format. For European accounts this is typically the IBAN; otherwise the domestic account number. Use the typed identifier fields (`iban`, `bban`, `maskedPan`) when you need to parse or route on it.

anyOf.description:
Type of bank account.

Aligned with the ISO 20022 ExternalCashAccountType1Code values exposed
via the Berlin Group PSD2 `cashAccountType`, collapsed to the buckets
that map cleanly across regions and reflect distinctions a user would
actually ask about:

  - `Current`  - day-to-day transaction account (ISO 20022 CACC).
                 Debit cards and stored-value / prepaid cards live
                 here too: `maskedPan` flags an attached card and the
                 balance/availableBalance pair answers "can I use it?"
  - `Savings`  - interest-bearing savings (ISO 20022 SVGS)
  - `Credit`   - revolving credit / credit card account; carries the
                 statement-cycle fields below (`statementBalance`,
                 `minimumPaymentDue`, `paymentDueDate`, ...).
  - `Loan`     - amortizing loan or mortgage (ISO 20022 LOAN)
  - `Other`    - anything else (escrow, brokerage, etc.)

availableBalance.description: Funds available to spend right now in `currency`, after pending authorisations and including any `overdraftLimit` headroom. Aligns with the PSD2 `interimAvailable` balance type.

balance.description: Current booked balance in `currency`. For credit accounts a negative value indicates an outstanding balance owed.

balanceUpdatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which `balance` and `availableBalance` were last refreshed by the bank.

items.description:
One typed balance entry on an account.

Profile of: Berlin Group PSD2 `balances` array element.

asOf.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this balance was last refreshed.

balances.description: Typed balance entries when the bank exposes more than just the booked and available scalars (`ClosingBooked`, `Expected`, `InterimAvailable`, `ForwardAvailable`, `NonInvoiced`). When populated, the top-level `balance` and `availableBalance` scalars are derived shortcuts for the most-recent `ClosingBooked` and `InterimAvailable` entries; servers MUST keep them consistent. Servers without typed balance support SHOULD omit this field and emit only the scalars.

bban.description: Basic Bank Account Number, the domestic identifier used in countries (or for products) without an IBAN.

bic.description: BIC / SWIFT code of the holding bank (ISO 9362).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the account. For multi-currency accounts (per PSD2 convention) use `XXX`.

iban.description: IBAN (ISO 13616), when the account has one.

isDefaultAccount.description: Whether this is the user's default account for new transfers. At most one account per response SHOULD have this set to true.

isWithdrawalAccount.description: Whether this account can be used as the source of an outgoing transfer or withdrawal.

maskedPan.description: Masked Primary Account Number for card accounts. The middle digits MUST be obscured.

minimumPaymentDue.description: Credit accounts only. Minimum payment required by `paymentDueDate` to keep the account current and avoid late fees, in `currency`. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.

name.description: Human-readable account name set by the bank or the user.

openedDate.description: Date the account was opened, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

overdraftLimit.description: Approved overdraft on a current/savings account or credit limit on a credit/card account, in `currency`. `0` (or omitted) means no extra headroom beyond `balance`.

ownerName.description: Primary account holder's name as the bank holds it. For joint accounts, servers MAY join multiple names with `, `.

paymentDueDate.description: Credit accounts only. ISO 8601 date by which `minimumPaymentDue` must be paid. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.

product.description: Bank's marketing name for the account product, per PSD2 `product`. Free-form and proprietary to the issuer.

statementBalance.description: Credit accounts only. Total billed on the most recent closed statement, in `currency`. Pay this in full by `paymentDueDate` to avoid interest charges. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.

statementClosingDate.description: Credit accounts only. ISO 8601 date the current billing cycle closes and the next statement is generated. Optional even on credit accounts; servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.

anyOf.description: Lifecycle status of an account, per Berlin Group PSD2 `accountStatus`.

status.description: Lifecycle status. If omitted, clients SHOULD treat the account as `Enabled`.

anyOf.description:
How the account is used, per Berlin Group PSD2 `usage`.

`Private` corresponds to PSD2 `PRIV` (personal); `Business` to `ORGA`
(professional / organisation).

usage.description: Whether this is a personal or business account.

description.description: Free-text shown on the counterparty's statement. Falls back to `remittanceInformation.unstructured` when both are present.

endToEndId.description: ISO 20022 cross-rail identifier. Server-generated when the client did not supply one; populated either way so the client can show / audit the value.

localInstrument.description: Rail-specific local instrument code (e.g., `INST` for SEPA Instant).

anyOf.description:
Free-text and/or structured remittance information for a transaction.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `RemittanceInformation`. Servers SHOULD populate
this only when remittance info is genuinely present and not just a
duplicate of `Transaction.description`.

creditorReference.description: Structured creditor reference, typically an ISO 11649 RF-prefixed reference. The reference is opaque to bank2ai; servers pass it through as the bank exposes it.

unstructured.description: Free-text remittance line (e.g., the SEPA `RemittanceInformation/Unstructured` field).

requestedExecutionDate.description: Requested execution date, ISO 8601. Omitted means as-soon-as-possible per the rail's defaults.

items.description:
Server-emitted advisory about a prepared transfer.

Used for AML hits, sanctions matches, unusual-amount notices, etc.
`severity: "block"` indicates the server will refuse `execute-transfer`
against this intent unless the user explicitly overrides.

warnings.description: Advisory or blocking warnings emitted during validation.

item.description: Prepared transfer details when validation succeeded.
